title: Non-Interactive Industrial Protocol Clients Without Device Identity

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for SR 1.2.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that IEC 62443 SR 1.2 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.15.12: Non-Interactive Industrial Protocol Clients Without Device Identity.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.15.12' running on index=ot_protocol Edge Hub edge_hub_modbus, edge_hub_opcua, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

app: Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151)
spl:
index=ot_protocol (sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" OR sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua") earliest=-24h
| eval client_key=coalesce(client_ip, opcua_client_appuri, modbus_unit_id)
| lookup ot_approved_clients.csv client_key OUTPUT asset_name, owner
| where isnull(asset_name)
| stats dc(function_code) as fc_dc, dc(node_id) as nodes, count by client_key, sourcetype
| sort - count
description: Finds Modbus/OPC-UA sessions where client identity cannot be tied to an approved automation asset, weakening software process and device identification.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for IEC 62443 compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes ot_protocol and sourcetypes edge_hub_modbus, edge_hub_opcua. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Build authoritative client inventory from engineering CMDB; (2) enrich Edge Hub events with IP→asset; (3) alert on new clients; (4) certificate-pin OPC-UA where possible. Indexes required: `index=ot_protocol`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=edge_hub_modbus`, `sourcetype=edge_hub_opcua`; Lookups: `ot_approved_clients`, `ot_approved_clients.csv`.
visualization: Table (unknown clients), Map (optional), Time chart (first seen). Key fields: `client_key`, `fc_dc`, `nodes`, `sourcetype`.
knownFalsePositives: Zone communications during authorized engineering, vendor maintenance, or planned commissioning. PAM sessions from authorized engineering access, vendor remote support, or scheduled maintenance.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=ot_protocol` Edge Hub `edge_hub_modbus`, `edge_hub_opcua`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Build authoritative client inventory from engineering CMDB; (2) enrich Edge Hub events with IP→asset; (3) alert on new clients; (4) certificate-pin OPC-UA where possible.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=ot_protocol (sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus" OR sourcetype="edge_hub_opcua") earliest=-24h
| eval client_key=coalesce(client_ip, opcua_client_appuri, modbus_unit_id)
| lookup ot_approved_clients.csv client_key OUTPUT asset_name, owner
| where isnull(asset_name)
| stats dc(function_code) as fc_dc, dc(node_id) as nodes, count by client_key, sourcetype
| sort - count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Non-Interactive Industrial Protocol Clients Without Device Identity (IEC 62443-3-3 / SR 1.2)** — Finds Modbus/OPC-UA sessions where client identity cannot be tied to an approved automation asset, weakening software process and device identification.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=ot_protocol` Edge Hub `edge_hub_modbus`, `edge_hub_opcua`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Edge Hub, Splunk OT Security Add-on (5151). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: ot_protocol; **sourcetype**: edge_hub_modbus, edge_hub_opcua. Those sourcetypes align with what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=ot_protocol, sourcetype="edge_hub_modbus", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **client_key** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Enriches events using `lookup` (lookup definition + optional OUTPUT fields).
• Filters the current rows with `where isnull(asset_name)` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by client_key, sourcetype** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the OT engineering record and session recording the control owner would use in an investigation. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (unknown clients), Map (optional), Time chart (first seen).
grandmaExplanation: We find Modbus/OPC-UA sessions where client identity cannot be tied to an approved automation asset, weakening software process and device identification. That helps protect factories, plants, and the gear that runs them from mistakes and bad actors the standards call out.
